description_long: This sconce consists of three distinct geometric frames—two square and one hexagonal—each crafted from wood with visible grain patterns. Each frame houses a single exposed light bulb, with the bulbs mounted on simple metallic or black fixtures and connected by visible cords. The arrangement of the frames is linear, with the largest square frame on one end, the hexagonal frame in the center, and the smallest square frame on the opposite end. The wood appears to be stained in varying shades, from dark brown to a lighter, natural finish, giving a rustic yet modern aesthetic. The bulbs are classic incandescent-style, with clear glass and visible filaments, contributing to an industrial vibe. The overall design is minimalist, with clean lines and a focus on geometric shapes.
description: A trio of geometric wooden frames—two squares and one hexagon—each with an exposed bulb and visible cord, blending rustic wood with industrial lighting elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three frames—large square, hexagon, and small square—with exposed bulbs and visible cords.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the frames and the alignment of the bulbs within each geometric shape.
description_view_2: Reverse front view with the same geometric arrangement, showing the backs of the bulbs and the wood grain details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, emphasizing the thickness of the wooden frames and the protruding bulbs.
attribute_fixture_design: The fixture features a minimalist, geometric design with rustic wood and industrial exposed bulbs, blending modern and vintage influences. The use of clean lines and natural materials gives it a contemporary yet warm appearance.
